\vspace{10pt}

June 21, 2026

\vspace{2pt}
Hiring Team, Sierra --- Agent Development --- New York, NY

\vspace{6pt}
Dear Hiring Team,

Sierra is betting that customer-facing AI agents have to be reliable enough to
\emph{act} on a company's behalf --- processing claims, returns, and refinances at
Fortune~500 scale --- not just chat. That is the exact problem I spent this past
summer building, which is why I am applying for the Agent Development internship.
At New York Life I shipped an AI voice-and-text agent that handled insurance
claims for 12,000 customer-service representatives --- one of the very workflows
agents built on Sierra now run.

At New York Life, those 12,000 reps were buried in claims information fragmented
across SharePoint, Confluence, Jira, and mainframe systems, so answers were slow
and inconsistent. I built an AI agent grounded in a 13,000-document knowledge base
I assembled from those sources, giving reps one trustworthy place to act. At Lykke
I went deeper on the reliability problem itself, engineering RAG pipelines with
citation events and token-budgeting so the model stays grounded over large, messy
corpora. Making agents trustworthy enough to take real action over enterprise data
is the problem I keep choosing to work on.

Beyond any single project, my real edge is how I work. I am fast and fluent with
modern AI tools and use them to ship production systems rather than demos, and I
pick up unfamiliar stacks and domains quickly --- this year alone I moved from
enterprise insurance automation to RAG infrastructure to CUDA robot motion planning
without losing momentum. As Sierra grows --- fresh off a \$950M raise, past \$150M
in ARR, and shipping Agent Studio~2.0 and the Agent Data Platform --- that is
exactly the leverage I bring: I can help the team move faster now and expand into
new agent surfaces next, because building with AI is my default mode, not a skill I
am still learning.

I would love to help Sierra build agents the Fortune~500 can trust to act. Thank
you for your time and consideration --- I would welcome the chance to talk.

\vspace{6pt}
Sincerely, \\
\textbf{Akash Dubey}
